text:
Four Saudi VLCCs have emerged this morning outside the Strait of Hormuz, with their draft indicating they are fully laden. 

Three of them briefly turned their AIS on a few days ago in the area of Ras Tanura oil port, before going dark, while the fourth went dark earlier.

And Saudi Aramco is loading more tankers inside the Strait of Hormuz / Persian Gulf. Satellite imagery from earlier today shows 3 tankers taking crude in the Sea Islands at Ras Tanura and the SPMs at Al Juaima'h

text: WTI prompt time-spread flips into contango
quoted_text: WTI prompt time-spread flirting with contango.

text:
The largest US egg producers conspired to manipulate the benchmark price used by the industry. 

Another reminder that some corners of the physical commodity market remain opaque and are vulnerable to manipulation.

US DoJ statement (and my highlights): https://t.co/BMSjesrrZv https://t.co/V1hQCWocuu

Saudi Arabia is pushing barrels both via the pipeline that bypasses the Strait of Hormuz (and loadings are higher now than a few weeks ago) plus in recent days it has started loading inside the Persian Gulf. The result? Lots of oil (and most of it is fresh loadings, not stocks).
quoted_text: Saudi Arabia’s crude oil exports have surged to roughly pre-war levels since the kingdom resumed cargo loadings inside the Persian Gulf https://t.co/JK0AUaQ7QY

text:
So far, it's a rush to export more and more oil.

For sure, some barrels are coming from inventories. But the export surge is steadily supported by rising wellhead output.

Soon, it would be a battle for market share. At that point, we would know if it turns into a price war.
